---
title: "figure1_summary"
author: "Ruwen Zhou"
date: "11/8/2024"
output: html_document
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE)
library(readr)
library(plyr)
library(tidyverse)
library(dplyr)
library(readxl)
library(ggplot2)
library(phyloseq)
library(vegan)
library(DESeq2)
library(pheatmap)
library(RColorBrewer)
library(writexl)
```


```{r}
helios_core_old = read_excel("data/HELIOS_Core_redacted.xlsx")
helios_core_new = read.csv("data/HELIOS2_Core_redacted_300.csv")
helios_core_new$FREG5_Ethnic_Group <- ifelse(helios_core_new$FREG5_Race == "CHINESE", "C",
                                             ifelse(helios_core_new$FREG5_Race == "INDIAN", "I",
                                             ifelse(helios_core_new$FREG5_Race == "MALAY", "M",
                                             ifelse(helios_core_new$FREG5_Race == "Other", "O", NA))))
helios_core_new$FREG7_Gender <- ifelse(helios_core_new$FREG7_Gender == "male", "M",
                                       ifelse(helios_core_new$FREG7_Gender == "female", "F",NA))
                                             

#helios_core_old
old_all = helios_core_old[,c("FREG0_PID", "FREG1_Barcode", "FREG5_Ethnic_Group", "FREG7_Gender", "FREG8_Age", "DBI13_Height", "DBI14_Weight", "FWH16_Waist1", "FWH19_Hip1", "FBP16_Caffeine", "FBP17_Smoking", "DBP9_Sbp_1", "DBP12_Dbp_1","DBP15_Hr_1")]
new_all = helios_core_new[,c("FREG0_PID", "FREG1_Barcode", "FREG5_Ethnic_Group", "FREG7_Gender", "FREG8_Age", "DBI13_Height", "DBI14_Weight", "FWH16_Waist1", "FWH19_Hip1", "FBP16_Caffeine", "FBP17_Smoking", "DBP9_SBP_1","DBP12_DBP_1", "DBP15_HR_1")]

```
"FREG0_PID", "FREG1_Barcode"

```{r}
library(writexl)

colnames(old_all) <- tolower(colnames(old_all))
colnames(new_all) <- tolower(colnames(new_all))

helios800 = rbind(old_all, new_all)
helios800
#write_xlsx(helios800, "data/helios800.xlsx")
```
```{r}
metadata_micro = read_csv("data/metadata_fil_01032024_redacted.csv")
sample_list = metadata_micro$`Original Sample name`
helios_new800 <- filter(helios800, freg1_barcode %in% sample_list)
helios_new800
```



```{r}
helios_new800$bmi = helios_new800$dbi14_weight/(helios_new800$dbi13_height*0.01)^2
helios_new800$bmi_groups <- cut(helios_new800$bmi, breaks = c(0,18.5,23,27.5,Inf),
                  labels = c("Underweight", "Normal", "Overweight", "Obese"),
                  include.lowest = TRUE, right = FALSE)
helios_new800
#write_xlsx(helios_new800, "data/summary_800.xlsx")
helios2 <- helios_new800 %>%
  dplyr::filter(freg5_ethnic_group != "O")
table(helios2$bmi_groups)
table(helios2$freg5_ethnic_group)
helios_new800
```
#demographic table stratified by ethnic
```{r}
cont_summary <- helios_new800 %>%
  dplyr::filter(freg5_ethnic_group != "O") %>%
  dplyr::group_by(freg5_ethnic_group) %>%
  dplyr::summarise(
    n = dplyr::n(),
    age_mean = mean(freg8_age, na.rm = TRUE),
    age_sd   = sd(freg8_age, na.rm = TRUE),
    bmi_mean = mean(bmi, na.rm = TRUE),
    bmi_sd   = sd(bmi, na.rm = TRUE),
    waist_mean = mean(fwh16_waist1, na.rm = TRUE),
    waist_sd   = sd(fwh16_waist1, na.rm = TRUE),
    hip_mean = mean(fwh19_hip1, na.rm = TRUE),
    hip_sd = sd(fwh19_hip1, na.rm = TRUE),
    sbp_mean = mean(dbp9_sbp_1, na.rm = TRUE),
    sbp_sd = sd(dbp9_sbp_1, na.rm = TRUE),
    dbp_mean = mean(dbp12_dbp_1, na.rm = TRUE),
    dbp_sd = sd(dbp12_dbp_1, na.rm = TRUE),
    hr_mean = mean(dbp15_hr_1, na.rm = TRUE),
    hr_sd = sd(dbp15_hr_1, na.rm = TRUE),
    height_mean = mean(dbi13_height, na.rm = TRUE),
    height_sd = sd(dbi13_height, na.rm = TRUE),
    weight_mean = mean(dbi14_weight, na.rm = TRUE),
    weight_sd = sd(dbi14_weight, na.rm = TRUE),
    .groups = "drop"
  )
cont_summary
bmi_group_summary <- helios_new800 %>%
  dplyr::filter(freg5_ethnic_group != "O") %>%
  dplyr::count(freg5_ethnic_group, bmi_groups, name = "n") %>%
  dplyr::group_by(freg5_ethnic_group) %>%
  dplyr::mutate(
    percent = round(100 * n / sum(n), 1),
    n_pct = paste0(n, " (", percent, "%)")
  ) %>%
  dplyr::ungroup()

bmi_group_summary

```
```{r}
library(dplyr)
library(tidyr)

bmi_group_wide <- bmi_group_summary %>%
  select(freg5_ethnic_group, bmi_groups, n_pct) %>%
  pivot_wider(
    names_from  = freg5_ethnic_group,
    values_from = n_pct
  ) %>%
  mutate(Variable = paste0("BMI group: ", bmi_groups)) %>%
  select(Variable, everything(), -bmi_groups)

```

```{r}
library(dplyr)
library(tidyr)
library(writexl)

## -------------------------
## 1) Continuous table (mean ± SD) + N
## -------------------------

# N row
n_table <- cont_summary %>%
  select(freg5_ethnic_group, n) %>%
  mutate(Variable = "N") %>%
  select(Variable, freg5_ethnic_group, n) %>%
  pivot_wider(names_from = freg5_ethnic_group, values_from = n)

# mean ± SD rows
cont_table <- cont_summary %>%
  select(-n) %>%  # remove n so it doesn't break mean/sd pairing
  pivot_longer(
    cols = -freg5_ethnic_group,
    names_to = "metric",
    values_to = "value"
  ) %>%
  mutate(
    var  = sub("_(mean|sd)$", "", metric),
    stat = ifelse(grepl("_mean$", metric), "mean", "sd")
  ) %>%
  select(-metric) %>%
  pivot_wider(names_from = stat, values_from = value) %>%
  mutate(cell = sprintf("%.2f ± %.2f", mean, sd)) %>%
  select(freg5_ethnic_group, var, cell) %>%
  pivot_wider(names_from = freg5_ethnic_group, values_from = cell) %>%
  mutate(
    Variable = dplyr::recode(
      var,
      age    = "Age (years)",
      bmi    = "BMI (kg/m²)",
      waist  = "Waist circumference",
      hip    = "Hip circumference",
      sbp    = "Systolic BP",
      dbp    = "Diastolic BP",
      hr     = "Heart rate",
      height = "Height",
      weight = "Weight",
      .default = var
    )
  ) %>%
  select(Variable, everything(), -var)

## -------------------------
## 2) BMI groups table (n (%))
## -------------------------
bmi_table <- bmi_group_summary %>%
  select(freg5_ethnic_group, bmi_groups, n_pct) %>%
  pivot_wider(names_from = freg5_ethnic_group, values_from = n_pct) %>%
  mutate(Variable = paste0("BMI group: ", bmi_groups)) %>%
  select(Variable, everything(), -bmi_groups)

## -------------------------
## 3) Final combined table
## -------------------------
n_table_chr <- n_table %>%
  mutate(across(-Variable, as.character))

supp_table <- dplyr::bind_rows(
  n_table_chr,
  cont_table,
  bmi_table
)

# Optional: enforce column order if your ethnic labels are C/I/M
supp_table <- supp_table %>%
  select(Variable, C, I, M, everything())
supp_table
## -------------------------
## 4) Export to Excel
## -------------------------
write_xlsx(
  x = list("Supp_Table" = supp_table),
  path = "Supplementary_Table_1_Ethnicity_Summary.xlsx"
)

```


```{r}
# Load required libraries
library(ggplot2)
library(dplyr)
library(tidyr)

# Define custom ethnic colors and single colors for other variables
ethnicity_colors <- c("C" = "#9FDDCA", "I" = "#9EA3F1", "M" = "#E17887", "O" = "#80796BFF")
variable_colors <- c(
  "age" = "#B19CD9",        # Purple for age
  "bmi" = "#FFB6C1",        # Light pink for BMI
  "waist" = "#FFE4B5",      # Light orange for waist
  "hip" = "#90EE90",        # Light green for hip
  "dbp" = "#ADD8E6",        # Light blue for DBP
  "sbp" = "#FFB366",        # Orange for SBP
  "hr" = "#D3D3D3"          # Light gray for HR
)

# Function to create individual-level bar plots
create_individual_plot <- function(data, var_name, use_ethnic_colors = FALSE) {
  base_plot <- ggplot(data, aes(x = 1:nrow(data), y = 1)) +
    labs(x = "Participants", 
         y = paste0(gsub("_", " ", var_name)),
         title = gsub("_", " ", var_name)) +
    theme_minimal() +
    theme(
      panel.grid.major.x = element_blank(),
      panel.grid.minor.x = element_blank(),
      axis.text.x = element_blank(),
      axis.ticks.x = element_blank(),
      legend.title = element_blank(),
      plot.margin = margin(t = 5, r = 10, b = 5, l = 10)
    )
    
  if (use_ethnic_colors) {
    base_plot + 
      geom_col(aes(fill = freg5_ethnic_group), width = 1) +
      scale_fill_manual(values = ethnicity_colors)
  } else {
    var_type <- case_when(
      grepl("age", var_name) ~ "age",
      grepl("bmi", var_name) ~ "bmi",
      grepl("waist", var_name) ~ "waist",
      grepl("hip", var_name) ~ "hip",
      grepl("dbp_1$", var_name) ~ "dbp",
      grepl("sbp", var_name) ~ "sbp",
      grepl("hr", var_name) ~ "hr",
      TRUE ~ "default"
    )
    
    if(var_name == "y") {  # For actual measurements
      base_plot + 
        geom_col(aes(y = .data[[var_name]]), width = 1, fill = variable_colors[var_type])
    } else {  # For gender
      base_plot + 
        geom_col(aes(fill = freg7_gender), width = 1) +
        scale_fill_manual(values = c("male" = "#0072B2", "female" = "#CC79A7"))
    }
  }
}

# Helper function to get units for variables
get_unit <- function(var_name) {
  units <- list(
    freg8_age = "years",
    bmi = "kg/m^2",
    fwh16_waist1 = "cm",
    fwh19_hip1 = "cm",
    dbp9_sbp_1 = "mmHg",
    dbp12_dbp_1 = "mmHg",
    dbp15_hr_1 = "bpm"
  )
  return(units[[var_name]] %||% "")
}

# Function to create all individual plots
create_all_individual_plots <- function(data) {
  # Sort data by ethnic group
  data <- data %>% arrange(freg5_ethnic_group)
  
  # Create list of plots
  plots <- list(
    ethnicity_plot = create_individual_plot(data, "freg5_ethnic_group", use_ethnic_colors = TRUE),
    age_plot = create_individual_plot(data, "freg8_age"),
    bmi_plot = create_individual_plot(data, "bmi"),
    waist_plot = create_individual_plot(data, "fwh16_waist1"),
    hip_plot = create_individual_plot(data, "fwh19_hip1"),
    dbp_plot = create_individual_plot(data, "dbp12_dbp_1"),
    sbp_plot = create_individual_plot(data, "dbp9_sbp_1"),
    hr_plot = create_individual_plot(data, "dbp15_hr_1")
  )
  
  # Create gender plot with colors
  plots$gender_plot <- ggplot(data, aes(x = 1:nrow(data), y = 1, fill = freg7_gender)) +
    geom_col(width = 1) +
    scale_fill_manual(values = c("male" = "#0072B2", "female" = "#CC79A7")) +
    labs(x = "Participants", y = "", title = "Gender") +
    theme_minimal() +
    theme(
      panel.grid.major.x = element_blank(),
      panel.grid.minor.x = element_blank(),
      axis.text.x = element_blank(),
      axis.text.y = element_blank(),
      axis.ticks.x = element_blank(),
      legend.title = element_blank(),
      plot.margin = margin(t = 5, r = 10, b = 5, l = 10)
    )
  
  return(plots)
}

# Example usage:
# First make sure your data is properly sorted
helios_new800 <- helios_new800 %>% arrange(freg5_ethnic_group)

# Create all plots
plots <- create_all_individual_plots(helios_new800)

# To combine all plots vertically:
library(patchwork)
combined_plot <- plots$ethnicity_plot / plots$age_plot / plots$bmi_plot / 
                plots$waist_plot / plots$hip_plot / plots$dbp_plot / 
                plots$sbp_plot / plots$hr_plot / plots$gender_plot +
                plot_layout(heights = rep(1, 9))

# Save the combined plot
#ggsave("figure/combined_plots.png", combined_plot, height = 22, width = 10)
```

```{r}
# Load required libraries
library(ggplot2)
library(dplyr)
library(tidyr)

# Define custom ethnic colors and single colors for other variables
ethnicity_colors <- c("C" = "#9FDDCA", "I" = "#9EA3F1", "M" = "#E17887", "O" = "#80796BFF")
gender_colors <- c("F" = "#CC79A7", "M" = "#0072B2")  # Define specific gender colors
variable_colors <- c(
  "age" = "#B19CD9",        # Purple for age
  "bmi" = "#FFB6C1",        # Light pink for BMI
  "waist" = "#FFE4B5",      # Light orange for waist
  "hip" = "#90EE90",        # Light green for hip
  "dbp" = "#ADD8E6",        # Light blue for DBP
  "sbp" = "#FFB366",        # Orange for SBP
  "hr" = "#D3D3D3"          # Light gray for HR
)

# Function to create individual-level bar plots
create_individual_plot <- function(data, var_name, use_ethnic_colors = FALSE) {
  if (use_ethnic_colors) {
    # For ethnicity plot
    p <- ggplot(data, aes(x = 1:nrow(data), y = 1, fill = freg5_ethnic_group)) +
      geom_col(width = 1) +
      scale_fill_manual(values = ethnicity_colors)
  } else if (var_name == "gender") {
    # For gender plot with fixed height and categorical colors
    p <- ggplot(data, aes(x = 1:nrow(data), y = 1, fill = freg7_gender)) +
      geom_col(width = 1) +
      scale_fill_manual(values = gender_colors)
  } else {
    # For all other variables
    p <- ggplot(data, aes(x = 1:nrow(data), y = .data[[var_name]])) +
      geom_col(width = 1, fill = case_when(
        var_name == "freg8_age" ~ variable_colors["age"],
        var_name == "bmi" ~ variable_colors["bmi"],
        var_name == "fwh16_waist1" ~ variable_colors["waist"],
        var_name == "fwh19_hip1" ~ variable_colors["hip"],
        var_name == "dbp12_dbp_1" ~ variable_colors["dbp"],
        var_name == "dbp9_sbp_1" ~ variable_colors["sbp"],
        var_name == "dbp15_hr_1" ~ variable_colors["hr"]
      ))
  }

  # Add common theme elements
  p + labs(x = "Participants", 
         y = paste0(gsub("_", " ", var_name), " (", get_unit(var_name), ")"),
         title = gsub("_", " ", var_name)) +
    theme_minimal() +
    theme(
      panel.grid.major.x = element_blank(),
      panel.grid.minor.x = element_blank(),
      axis.text.x = element_blank(),
      axis.ticks.x = element_blank(),
      legend.title = element_blank(),
      plot.margin = margin(t = 5, r = 10, b = 5, l = 10)
    ) +
    if(var_name == "gender" || use_ethnic_colors) {
      # Remove y-axis text for categorical plots
      theme(axis.text.y = element_blank())
    } else {
      theme()
    }
}

# Helper function to get units for variables
get_unit <- function(var_name) {
  units <- list(
    freg8_age = "years",
    bmi = "kg/m^2",
    fwh16_waist1 = "cm",
    fwh19_hip1 = "cm",
    dbp9_sbp_1 = "mmHg",
    dbp12_dbp_1 = "mmHg",
    dbp15_hr_1 = "bpm"
  )
  return(units[[var_name]] %||% "")
}

# Function to create all individual plots
create_all_individual_plots <- function(data) {
  # Sort data by ethnic group
  data <- data %>% 
    arrange(freg5_ethnic_group) %>%
    # Ensure gender is F/M format
    mutate(freg7_gender = toupper(substr(freg7_gender, 1, 1)))
  
  # Create list of plots
  plots <- list(
    ethnicity_plot = create_individual_plot(data, "freg5_ethnic_group", use_ethnic_colors = TRUE),
    age_plot = create_individual_plot(data, "freg8_age"),
    bmi_plot = create_individual_plot(data, "bmi"),
    waist_plot = create_individual_plot(data, "fwh16_waist1"),
    hip_plot = create_individual_plot(data, "fwh19_hip1"),
    dbp_plot = create_individual_plot(data, "dbp12_dbp_1"),
    sbp_plot = create_individual_plot(data, "dbp9_sbp_1"),
    hr_plot = create_individual_plot(data, "dbp15_hr_1"),
    gender_plot = create_individual_plot(data, "gender")
  )
  
  return(plots)
}

# Example usage:
# First make sure your data is properly sorted
helios_new800 <- helios_new800 %>% arrange(freg5_ethnic_group)

# Create all plots
plots <- create_all_individual_plots(helios_new800)

# To combine all plots vertically:
library(patchwork)
combined_plot <- plots$ethnicity_plot / plots$age_plot / plots$bmi_plot / 
                plots$waist_plot / plots$hip_plot / plots$dbp_plot / 
                plots$sbp_plot / plots$hr_plot / plots$gender_plot +
                plot_layout(heights = rep(1, 9))

# Save the combined plot
#ggsave("figure/combined_plots.png", combined_plot, height = 22, width = 10)
```

```{r}
plot_info <- list(
  list(name = "ethnicity_plot", filename = "figure/figure1_summary/ethnicity_distribution.pdf"),
  list(name = "age_plot", filename = "figure/figure1_summary/age_distribution.pdf"),
  list(name = "bmi_plot", filename = "figure/figure1_summary/bmi_distribution.pdf"),
  list(name = "waist_plot", filename = "figure/figure1_summary/waist_distribution.pdf"),
  list(name = "hip_plot", filename = "figure/figure1_summary/hip_distribution.pdf"),
  list(name = "dbp_plot", filename = "figure/figure1_summary/dbp_distribution.pdf"),
  list(name = "sbp_plot", filename = "figure/figure1_summary/sbp_distribution.pdf"),
  list(name = "hr_plot", filename = "figure/figure1_summary/hr_distribution.pdf"),
  list(name = "gender_plot", filename = "figure/figure1_summary/gender_distribution.pdf")
)

# Save individual plots as PDF (vector) files
for (plot_item in plot_info) {
  ggsave(
    filename = plot_item$filename,
    plot = plots[[plot_item$name]],
    width = 10,
    height = 3,
    device = cairo_pdf  # Explicitly use cairo_pdf for high-quality vector output
  )
}
```

```{r}
# Load required libraries
library(ggplot2)
library(dplyr)
library(gghalves)

# Define the same colors as before for consistency
variable_colors <- c(
  "age" = "#B19CD9",        # Purple for age
  "bmi" = "#FFB6C1",        # Light pink for BMI
  "waist" = "#FFE4B5",      # Light orange for waist
  "hip" = "#90EE90",        # Light green for hip
  "dbp" = "#ADD8E6",        # Light blue for DBP
  "sbp" = "#FFB366",        # Orange for SBP
  "hr" = "#D3D3D3"          # Light gray for HR
)

# Create individual half violin + half box plots
# Age plot
plot_age <- helios_new800 %>%
  ggplot(aes(x = "Age", y = freg8_age)) +
  geom_half_violin(
    fill = variable_colors["age"],
    color = "black",
    alpha = 0.5
  ) +
  geom_half_boxplot(
    fill = variable_colors["age"],
    alpha = 0.5,
    color = "black",
    side = "r"
  ) +
  labs(x = "", y = "Age (years)") +
  theme_minimal()

# BMI plot
plot_bmi <- helios_new800 %>%
  ggplot(aes(x = "BMI", y = bmi)) +
  geom_half_violin(
    fill = variable_colors["bmi"],
    color = "black",
    alpha = 0.5
  ) +
  geom_half_boxplot(
    fill = variable_colors["bmi"],
    alpha = 0.5,
    color = "black",
    side = "r"
  ) +
  labs(x = "", y = "BMI (kg/m²)") +
  theme_minimal()

# Waist plot
plot_waist <- helios_new800 %>%
  ggplot(aes(x = "Waist", y = fwh16_waist1)) +
  geom_half_violin(
    fill = variable_colors["waist"],
    color = "black",
    alpha = 0.5
  ) +
  geom_half_boxplot(
    fill = variable_colors["waist"],
    alpha = 0.5,
    color = "black",
    side = "r"
  ) +
  labs(x = "", y = "Waist (cm)") +
  theme_minimal()

# Hip plot
plot_hip <- helios_new800 %>%
  ggplot(aes(x = "Hip", y = fwh19_hip1)) +
  geom_half_violin(
    fill = variable_colors["hip"],
    color = "black",
    alpha = 0.5
  ) +
  geom_half_boxplot(
    fill = variable_colors["hip"],
    alpha = 0.5,
    color = "black",
    side = "r"
  ) +
  labs(x = "", y = "Hip (cm)") +
  theme_minimal()

# SBP plot
plot_sbp <- helios_new800 %>%
  ggplot(aes(x = "SBP", y = dbp9_sbp_1)) +
  geom_half_violin(
    fill = variable_colors["sbp"],
    color = "black",
    alpha = 0.5
  ) +
  geom_half_boxplot(
    fill = variable_colors["sbp"],
    alpha = 0.5,
    color = "black",
    side = "r"
  ) +
  labs(x = "", y = "SBP (mmHg)") +
  theme_minimal()

# DBP plot
plot_dbp <- helios_new800 %>%
  ggplot(aes(x = "DBP", y = dbp12_dbp_1)) +
  geom_half_violin(
    fill = variable_colors["dbp"],
    color = "black",
    alpha = 0.5
  ) +
  geom_half_boxplot(
    fill = variable_colors["dbp"],
    alpha = 0.5,
    color = "black",
    side = "r"
  ) +
  labs(x = "", y = "DBP (mmHg)") +
  theme_minimal()

# HR plot
plot_hr <- helios_new800 %>%
  ggplot(aes(x = "HR", y = dbp15_hr_1)) +
  geom_half_violin(
    fill = variable_colors["hr"],
    color = "black",
    alpha = 0.5
  ) +
  geom_half_boxplot(
    fill = variable_colors["hr"],
    alpha = 0.5,
    color = "black",
    side = "r"
  ) +
  labs(x = "", y = "HR (bpm)") +
  theme_minimal()

# Combine all plots using patchwork
library(patchwork)
combined_violin_plots <- plot_bmi + plot_age +  plot_waist + 
                        plot_hip + plot_sbp + plot_dbp + 
                        plot_hr +
                        plot_layout(ncol = 7, widths = rep(1, 7))


ggsave("figure/figure1_summary/combined_violin_plots.pdf", combined_violin_plots, 
       width = 15, height = 6)
```

## life style
```{r}
helios_life = read_excel("data/summary_800.xlsx")
helios_life
```

```{r}
# For caffeine consumption by ethnic group
ggplot(helios_life %>% 
       filter(!is.na(freg5_ethnic_group)), # Remove NA from ethnic groups
       aes(x = freg5_ethnic_group, fill = fbp16_caffeine)) +
  geom_bar(position = "dodge") +
  labs(x = "Ethnic Group", 
       y = "Count",
       fill = "Caffeine Consumption",
       title = "Caffeine Consumption by Ethnic Group") +
  theme_minimal() +
  scale_fill_brewer(palette = "Blues")

# For smoking frequency by ethnic group 
ggplot(helios_life %>% 
       filter(!is.na(freg5_ethnic_group)), # Remove NA from ethnic groups
       aes(x = freg5_ethnic_group, fill = fbp17_smoking)) +
  geom_bar(position = "dodge") +
  labs(x = "Ethnic Group", 
       y = "Count",
       fill = "Smoking Frequency",
       title = "Smoking Frequency by Ethnic Group") +
  theme_minimal() +
  scale_fill_brewer(palette = "Oranges")
```
```{r}
# For proportional representation
ggplot(helios_life, aes(x = freg5_ethnic_group, fill = fbp16_caffeine)) +
  geom_bar(position = "fill") +
  scale_y_continuous(labels = scales::percent) +
  labs(x = "Ethnic Group", 
       y = "Percentage",
       fill = "Caffeine Consumption",
       title = "Proportion of Caffeine Consumption by Ethnic Group") +
  theme_minimal()
```

```{r}
# Load required libraries
library(ggplot2)
library(dplyr)
library(tidyr)

# Create proportions and prepare data
plot_data <- helios_life %>%
  filter(!is.na(freg5_ethnic_group)) %>%
  group_by(freg5_ethnic_group, fbp16_caffeine) %>%
  summarise(count = n()) %>%
  group_by(freg5_ethnic_group) %>%
  mutate(prop = count/sum(count),
         # Create variables for donut chart positioning
         ymax = cumsum(prop),
         ymin = c(0, head(ymax, n=-1)),
         labelpos = (ymax + ymin)/2) %>%
  ungroup()

# Create the donut charts with faceting
ggplot(plot_data, aes(fill = fbp16_caffeine, ymax = ymax, ymin = ymin, xmax = 4, xmin = 3)) +
  geom_rect(aes(ymax = ymax, ymin = ymin, xmax = 4, xmin = 3)) +
  coord_polar(theta = "y") +
  facet_wrap(~freg5_ethnic_group, ncol = 2) +
  scale_fill_brewer(palette = "Blues") +
  theme_void() +
  labs(title = "Caffeine Consumption Distribution by Ethnic Group",
       fill = "Caffeine Consumption") +
  theme(legend.position = "bottom")

# For smoking data, similar approach:
plot_data_smoking <- helios_life %>%
  filter(!is.na(freg5_ethnic_group)) %>%
  group_by(freg5_ethnic_group, fbp17_smoking) %>%
  summarise(count = n()) %>%
  group_by(freg5_ethnic_group) %>%
  mutate(prop = count/sum(count),
         ymax = cumsum(prop),
         ymin = c(0, head(ymax, n=-1)),
         labelpos = (ymax + ymin)/2) %>%
  ungroup()

# Create donut charts for smoking
ggplot(plot_data_smoking, aes(fill = fbp17_smoking, ymax = ymax, ymin = ymin, xmax = 4, xmin = 3)) +
  geom_rect(aes(ymax = ymax, ymin = ymin, xmax = 4, xmin = 3)) +
  coord_polar(theta = "y") +
  facet_wrap(~freg5_ethnic_group, ncol = 2) +
  scale_fill_brewer(palette = "Oranges",
                    labels = c("Smoker", "Non-smoker")) +
  theme_void() +
  labs(title = "Smoking Distribution by Ethnic Group",
       fill = "Smoking") +
  theme(legend.position = "bottom")
```

## sumary table
```{r}
# Load required libraries
library(dplyr)
library(knitr)
library(kableExtra)
library(tidyr)

# Function to create summary statistics
create_summary_table <- function(df) {
  
  # Ensure df is a data frame
  if(!is.data.frame(df)) {
    if(is.list(df)) {
      df <- as.data.frame(df)
    } else {
      stop("Input must be a data frame or convertible to one")
    }
  }
  
  cat("Working with data frame of dimensions:", dim(df), "\n")
  
  # Categorical variables summary
  cat_summary <- list()
  
  # Gender summary (F/M)
  if("freg7_gender" %in% names(df)) {
    cat("Processing gender data...\n")
    gender_table <- table(df$freg7_gender, useNA = "ifany")
    gender_summary <- data.frame(
      Category = names(gender_table),
      Count = as.numeric(gender_table),
      Percentage = round(as.numeric(gender_table)/sum(gender_table)*100, 1)
    )
    cat_summary[["Gender"]] <- gender_summary
  }
  
  # Ethnic group summary (C/I/M/O)
  if("freg5_ethnic_group" %in% names(df)) {
    cat("Processing ethnicity data...\n")
    ethnic_table <- table(df$freg5_ethnic_group, useNA = "ifany")
    ethnic_summary <- data.frame(
      Category = names(ethnic_table),
      Count = as.numeric(ethnic_table),
      Percentage = round(as.numeric(ethnic_table)/sum(ethnic_table)*100, 1)
    )
    cat_summary[["Ethnic Group"]] <- ethnic_summary
  }
  
  # BMI groups summary (Normal, Overweight, Obese, Underweight)
  if("bmi_groups" %in% names(df)) {
    cat("Processing BMI groups data...\n")
    bmi_table <- table(df$bmi_groups, useNA = "ifany")
    bmi_summary <- data.frame(
      Category = names(bmi_table),
      Count = as.numeric(bmi_table),
      Percentage = round(as.numeric(bmi_table)/sum(bmi_table)*100, 1)
    )
    # Reorder if possible
    bmi_order <- c("Underweight", "Normal", "Overweight", "Obese")
    bmi_summary$Category <- factor(bmi_summary$Category, levels = bmi_order)
    bmi_summary <- bmi_summary[order(bmi_summary$Category), ]
    cat_summary[["BMI Groups"]] <- bmi_summary
  }
  
  # Caffeine consumption summary
  if("fbp16_caffeine" %in% names(df)) {
    cat("Processing caffeine data...\n")
    caffeine_table <- table(df$fbp16_caffeine, useNA = "ifany")
    caffeine_summary <- data.frame(
      Category = names(caffeine_table),
      Count = as.numeric(caffeine_table),
      Percentage = round(as.numeric(caffeine_table)/sum(caffeine_table)*100, 1)
    )
    cat_summary[["Caffeine Consumption"]] <- caffeine_summary
  }
  
  # Smoking status summary
  if("fbp17_smoking" %in% names(df)) {
    cat("Processing smoking data...\n")
    smoking_table <- table(df$fbp17_smoking, useNA = "ifany")
    smoking_summary <- data.frame(
      Category = names(smoking_table),
      Count = as.numeric(smoking_table),
      Percentage = round(as.numeric(smoking_table)/sum(smoking_table)*100, 1)
    )
    cat_summary[["Smoking Status"]] <- smoking_summary
  }
  
  # Numerical variables summary using base R
  numeric_vars <- c("freg8_age", "dbi13_height", "dbi14_weight", "fwh16_waist1", 
                    "fwh19_hip1", "dbp9_sbp_1", "dbp12_dbp_1", "dbp15_hr_1", "bmi")
  
  # Select only numeric variables that exist in the data
  existing_numeric_vars <- numeric_vars[numeric_vars %in% names(df)]
  
  if(length(existing_numeric_vars) > 0) {
    cat("Processing numerical variables...\n")
    numeric_summary <- data.frame(
      Variable = existing_numeric_vars,
      Mean = round(sapply(df[existing_numeric_vars], function(x) mean(x, na.rm = TRUE)), 2),
      Median = round(sapply(df[existing_numeric_vars], function(x) median(x, na.rm = TRUE)), 2),
      SD = round(sapply(df[existing_numeric_vars], function(x) sd(x, na.rm = TRUE)), 2),
      Min = round(sapply(df[existing_numeric_vars], function(x) min(x, na.rm = TRUE)), 2),
      Max = round(sapply(df[existing_numeric_vars], function(x) max(x, na.rm = TRUE)), 2),
      Missing = sapply(df[existing_numeric_vars], function(x) sum(is.na(x)))
    )
    rownames(numeric_summary) <- NULL
  } else {
    numeric_summary <- data.frame(Message = "No numeric variables found")
  }
  
  return(list(categorical = cat_summary, numerical = numeric_summary))
}

# Check the data structure first
cat("Checking data structure...\n")
cat("Class of helios_new800:", class(helios_new800), "\n")
cat("Dimensions:", dim(helios_new800), "\n")
cat("Column names:", names(helios_new800), "\n\n")

# Convert to data frame if needed
if(is.list(helios_new800) && !is.data.frame(helios_new800)) {
  helios_new800 <- as.data.frame(helios_new800)
  cat("Converted to data frame\n")
}

# Create the summary
summary_results <- create_summary_table(helios_new800)

# Print categorical summaries
cat("=== CATEGORICAL VARIABLES SUMMARY ===\n\n")
for(var_name in names(summary_results$categorical)) {
  cat(paste("**", var_name, "**\n"))
  print(kable(summary_results$categorical[[var_name]], 
              caption = paste(var_name, "Distribution")))
  cat("\n")
}

# Print numerical summary
cat("=== NUMERICAL VARIABLES SUMMARY ===\n")
print(kable(summary_results$numerical, 
            caption = "Descriptive Statistics for Numerical Variables",
            digits = 2))

# Alternative: Create a more compact summary table using base R
cat("\n=== COMPACT SUMMARY TABLE ===\n")
compact_summary <- data.frame(
  Statistic = c("Total_N", "Male_n", "Male_pct", "Female_n", "Female_pct", 
                "Age_mean", "Age_sd", "BMI_mean", "BMI_sd", "SBP_mean", "DBP_mean"),
  Value = c(
    nrow(helios_new800),
    sum(helios_new800$freg7_gender == "M", na.rm = TRUE),
    round(sum(helios_new800$freg7_gender == "M", na.rm = TRUE)/nrow(helios_new800)*100, 1),
    sum(helios_new800$freg7_gender == "F", na.rm = TRUE),
    round(sum(helios_new800$freg7_gender == "F", na.rm = TRUE)/nrow(helios_new800)*100, 1),
    round(mean(helios_new800$freg8_age, na.rm = TRUE), 1),
    round(sd(helios_new800$freg8_age, na.rm = TRUE), 1),
    round(mean(helios_new800$bmi, na.rm = TRUE), 1),
    round(sd(helios_new800$bmi, na.rm = TRUE), 1),
    round(mean(helios_new800$dbp9_sbp_1, na.rm = TRUE), 1),
    round(mean(helios_new800$dbp12_dbp_1, na.rm = TRUE), 1)
  )
)

print(kable(compact_summary, caption = "Overall Dataset Summary"))

# Create a publication-ready table using base R
cat("\n=== PUBLICATION-READY TABLE ===\n")
pub_table <- data.frame(
  Characteristic = c(
    "Total Participants",
    "Age, years (mean ± SD)",
    "Male, n (%)",
    "Female, n (%)",
    "BMI, kg/m² (mean ± SD)",
    "SBP, mmHg (mean ± SD)",
    "DBP, mmHg (mean ± SD)"
  ),
  Value = c(
    nrow(helios_new800),
    paste0(round(mean(helios_new800$freg8_age, na.rm = TRUE), 1), 
           " ± ", round(sd(helios_new800$freg8_age, na.rm = TRUE), 1)),
    paste0(sum(helios_new800$freg7_gender == "M", na.rm = TRUE), 
           " (", round(sum(helios_new800$freg7_gender == "M", na.rm = TRUE)/nrow(helios_new800)*100, 1), ")"),
    paste0(sum(helios_new800$freg7_gender == "F", na.rm = TRUE), 
           " (", round(sum(helios_new800$freg7_gender == "F", na.rm = TRUE)/nrow(helios_new800)*100, 1), ")"),
    paste0(round(mean(helios_new800$bmi, na.rm = TRUE), 1), 
           " ± ", round(sd(helios_new800$bmi, na.rm = TRUE), 1)),
    paste0(round(mean(helios_new800$dbp9_sbp_1, na.rm = TRUE), 1), 
           " ± ", round(sd(helios_new800$dbp9_sbp_1, na.rm = TRUE), 1)),
    paste0(round(mean(helios_new800$dbp12_dbp_1, na.rm = TRUE), 1), 
           " ± ", round(sd(helios_new800$dbp12_dbp_1, na.rm = TRUE), 1))
  )
)

print(kable(pub_table, caption = "Participant Characteristics"))
```
```{r}
# Export Summary Tables to Excel for Research Publication
# Install required packages if you don't have them:
# install.packages(c("openxlsx", "writexl"))

library(openxlsx)

# Run your summary function first
summary_results <- create_summary_table(helios_new800)

# Create a new workbook
wb <- createWorkbook()

# ============================================================
# Sheet 1: Publication-Ready Table (Main Table for Paper)
# ============================================================

# Create the main publication table
pub_table <- data.frame(
  Characteristic = c(
    "Total Participants, n",
    "Age, years (mean ± SD)",
    "Gender, n (%)",
    "  Male",
    "  Female",
    "Ethnicity, n (%)",
    "  Chinese",
    "  Indian", 
    "  Malay",
    "  Others",
    "BMI, kg/m² (mean ± SD)",
    "BMI Categories, n (%)",
    "  Underweight",
    "  Normal",
    "  Overweight", 
    "  Obese",
    "Clinical Measurements (mean ± SD)",
    "  Systolic BP, mmHg",
    "  Diastolic BP, mmHg",
    "  Heart Rate, bpm",
    "  Height, cm",
    "  Weight, kg",
    "  Waist circumference, cm",
    "  Hip circumference, cm"
  ),
  Value = character(24)
)

# Fill in the values
pub_table$Value[1] <- as.character(nrow(helios_new800))
pub_table$Value[2] <- paste0(round(mean(helios_new800$freg8_age, na.rm = TRUE), 1), 
                             " ± ", round(sd(helios_new800$freg8_age, na.rm = TRUE), 1))

# Gender
male_n <- sum(helios_new800$freg7_gender == "M", na.rm = TRUE)
female_n <- sum(helios_new800$freg7_gender == "F", na.rm = TRUE)
total_gender <- male_n + female_n
pub_table$Value[4] <- paste0(male_n, " (", round(male_n/total_gender*100, 1), ")")
pub_table$Value[5] <- paste0(female_n, " (", round(female_n/total_gender*100, 1), ")")

# Ethnicity (if available)
if("freg5_ethnic_group" %in% names(helios_new800)) {
  eth_table <- table(helios_new800$freg5_ethnic_group, useNA = "no")
  total_eth <- sum(eth_table)
  if("C" %in% names(eth_table)) pub_table$Value[7] <- paste0(eth_table["C"], " (", round(eth_table["C"]/total_eth*100, 1), ")")
  if("I" %in% names(eth_table)) pub_table$Value[8] <- paste0(eth_table["I"], " (", round(eth_table["I"]/total_eth*100, 1), ")")
  if("M" %in% names(eth_table)) pub_table$Value[9] <- paste0(eth_table["M"], " (", round(eth_table["M"]/total_eth*100, 1), ")")
  if("O" %in% names(eth_table)) pub_table$Value[10] <- paste0(eth_table["O"], " (", round(eth_table["O"]/total_eth*100, 1), ")")
}

# BMI
pub_table$Value[11] <- paste0(round(mean(helios_new800$bmi, na.rm = TRUE), 1), 
                              " ± ", round(sd(helios_new800$bmi, na.rm = TRUE), 1))

# BMI Categories (if available)
if("bmi_groups" %in% names(helios_new800)) {
  bmi_table <- table(helios_new800$bmi_groups, useNA = "no")
  total_bmi <- sum(bmi_table)
  if("Underweight" %in% names(bmi_table)) pub_table$Value[13] <- paste0(bmi_table["Underweight"], " (", round(bmi_table["Underweight"]/total_bmi*100, 1), ")")
  if("Normal" %in% names(bmi_table)) pub_table$Value[14] <- paste0(bmi_table["Normal"], " (", round(bmi_table["Normal"]/total_bmi*100, 1), ")")
  if("Overweight" %in% names(bmi_table)) pub_table$Value[15] <- paste0(bmi_table["Overweight"], " (", round(bmi_table["Overweight"]/total_bmi*100, 1), ")")
  if("Obese" %in% names(bmi_table)) pub_table$Value[16] <- paste0(bmi_table["Obese"], " (", round(bmi_table["Obese"]/total_bmi*100, 1), ")")
}

# Clinical measurements
pub_table$Value[18] <- paste0(round(mean(helios_new800$dbp9_sbp_1, na.rm = TRUE), 1), 
                              " ± ", round(sd(helios_new800$dbp9_sbp_1, na.rm = TRUE), 1))
pub_table$Value[19] <- paste0(round(mean(helios_new800$dbp12_dbp_1, na.rm = TRUE), 1), 
                              " ± ", round(sd(helios_new800$dbp12_dbp_1, na.rm = TRUE), 1))
pub_table$Value[20] <- paste0(round(mean(helios_new800$dbp15_hr_1, na.rm = TRUE), 1), 
                              " ± ", round(sd(helios_new800$dbp15_hr_1, na.rm = TRUE), 1))
pub_table$Value[21] <- paste0(round(mean(helios_new800$dbi13_height, na.rm = TRUE), 1), 
                              " ± ", round(sd(helios_new800$dbi13_height, na.rm = TRUE), 1))
pub_table$Value[22] <- paste0(round(mean(helios_new800$dbi14_weight, na.rm = TRUE), 1), 
                              " ± ", round(sd(helios_new800$dbi14_weight, na.rm = TRUE), 1))
pub_table$Value[23] <- paste0(round(mean(helios_new800$fwh16_waist1, na.rm = TRUE), 1), 
                              " ± ", round(sd(helios_new800$fwh16_waist1, na.rm = TRUE), 1))
pub_table$Value[24] <- paste0(round(mean(helios_new800$fwh19_hip1, na.rm = TRUE), 1), 
                              " ± ", round(sd(helios_new800$fwh19_hip1, na.rm = TRUE), 1))

# Add the main publication table
addWorksheet(wb, "Table1_Baseline_Characteristics")
writeData(wb, "Table1_Baseline_Characteristics", pub_table)

# Format the publication table
addStyle(wb, "Table1_Baseline_Characteristics", 
         style = createStyle(textDecoration = "bold"), 
         rows = 1, cols = 1:2)

# Add indentation for subcategories
indent_rows <- c(4, 5, 7, 8, 9, 10, 13, 14, 15, 16, 18, 19, 20, 21, 22, 23, 24)
addStyle(wb, "Table1_Baseline_Characteristics", 
         style = createStyle(indent = 1), 
         rows = indent_rows + 1, cols = 1)

# ============================================================
# Sheet 2: Detailed Categorical Variables
# ============================================================
addWorksheet(wb, "Categorical_Variables")

# Combine all categorical summaries
all_categorical <- data.frame()
for(var_name in names(summary_results$categorical)) {
  temp_df <- summary_results$categorical[[var_name]]
  temp_df$Variable <- var_name
  temp_df <- temp_df[, c("Variable", "Category", "Count", "Percentage")]
  all_categorical <- rbind(all_categorical, temp_df)
}

writeData(wb, "Categorical_Variables", all_categorical)
addStyle(wb, "Categorical_Variables", 
         style = createStyle(textDecoration = "bold"), 
         rows = 1, cols = 1:4)

# ============================================================
# Sheet 3: Numerical Variables Summary
# ============================================================
addWorksheet(wb, "Numerical_Variables")
writeData(wb, "Numerical_Variables", summary_results$numerical)
addStyle(wb, "Numerical_Variables", 
         style = createStyle(textDecoration = "bold"), 
         rows = 1, cols = 1:ncol(summary_results$numerical))

# ============================================================
# Sheet 4: Raw Data Summary (for reference)
# ============================================================
addWorksheet(wb, "Data_Overview")

# Create overview table
overview <- data.frame(
  Metric = c("Dataset Name", "Total Observations", "Total Variables", 
             "Missing Data Present", "Date Generated"),
  Value = c("helios_new800", 
            nrow(helios_new800), 
            ncol(helios_new800),
            ifelse(any(is.na(helios_new800)), "Yes", "No"),
            Sys.Date())
)

writeData(wb, "Data_Overview", overview)
addStyle(wb, "Data_Overview", 
         style = createStyle(textDecoration = "bold"), 
         rows = 1, cols = 1:2)

# Add variable list
writeData(wb, "Data_Overview", data.frame(Variable = names(helios_new800)), 
          startRow = 8, startCol = 1)
writeData(wb, "Data_Overview", "Variable List:", startRow = 7, startCol = 1)

# ============================================================
# Sheet 5: Missing Data Analysis (if needed)
# ============================================================
addWorksheet(wb, "Missing_Data")

# Create missing data summary
missing_summary <- data.frame(
  Variable = names(helios_new800),
  Missing_Count = sapply(helios_new800, function(x) sum(is.na(x))),
  Missing_Percentage = round(sapply(helios_new800, function(x) sum(is.na(x))/length(x)*100), 2),
  Complete_Cases = sapply(helios_new800, function(x) sum(!is.na(x)))
)
missing_summary$Total_N <- nrow(helios_new800)

writeData(wb, "Missing_Data", missing_summary)
addStyle(wb, "Missing_Data", 
         style = createStyle(textDecoration = "bold"), 
         rows = 1, cols = 1:5)

# ============================================================
# Save the Excel file
# ============================================================
saveWorkbook(wb, "Helios_Research_Tables.xlsx", overwrite = TRUE)

cat("✅ Excel file 'Helios_Research_Tables.xlsx' created successfully!\n\n")
cat("📊 The file contains 5 sheets:\n")
cat("   1. Table1_Baseline_Characteristics - Main table for your paper\n")
cat("   2. Categorical_Variables - Detailed breakdowns\n") 
cat("   3. Numerical_Variables - Descriptive statistics\n")
cat("   4. Data_Overview - Dataset information\n")
cat("   5. Missing_Data - Missing data analysis\n\n")
cat("📝 Sheet 1 is formatted for direct use in your research paper!\n")
cat("📍 File saved in your working directory: ", getwd(), "\n")

# Optional: Open the file automatically (Windows)
# shell.exec("Helios_Research_Tables.xlsx")

# Display quick preview of the main table
cat("\n📋 Preview of Publication Table (Table 1):\n")
print(head(pub_table, 10))
```
